features.description: A developer can configure, build, and run the standalone insight-io backend targets
steps: Run CMake configure from the repo root
Build the backend binaries and focused tests
Verify the expected binaries are produced
Run the focused test targets successfully

features.description: An operator can start the backend and verify the health endpoint
steps: Start the backend with a db path and frontend path
Call GET /api/health
Verify status is ok
Verify the backend version is returned

features.description: The device catalog lists derived URIs where each URI represents one fixed published source shape
steps: Call GET /api/devices
Inspect listed URI entries
Verify each URI corresponds to one exact member or one fixed grouped preset bundle
Verify route expectations are not required to disambiguate the URI

features.description: RGBD discovery exposes separate depth outputs when D2C changes delivered caps
steps: Call GET /api/devices for an RGBD camera
Verify exact URIs are listed for `orbbec/depth/400p_30` and `orbbec/depth/480p_30`
Verify the grouped preset URI `orbbec/preset/480p_30` is listed when the device supports that fixed bundled flow
Verify their metadata reports different delivered caps or capture policy

features.description: Stereo or dual-eye devices can expose selector-disambiguated exact URIs using `/channel/<name>` when needed
steps: Call GET /api/devices for a stereo device
Verify disambiguated channel URIs are listed
Verify the full final URI is available without manual composition

features.description: Related exact URIs from one source group either resolve to one compatible grouped runtime or the newer request is rejected
steps: Create one grouped-source session or route binding
Create a second related grouped-source request
Inspect runtime status
Verify the backend either reuses one compatible grouped runtime or rejects the newer request with a compatibility error

features.description: A real Orbbec device is tested to determine whether `orbbec/depth/480p_30` can run as the only user-requested stream, and whether `orbbec/preset/480p_30` works as a stable grouped preset
steps: Connect one real Orbbec RGBD device
Verify discovery lists `orbbec/color/480p_30`, `orbbec/depth/400p_30`, `orbbec/depth/480p_30`, and `orbbec/preset/480p_30` when supported
Start only `orbbec/depth/480p_30` through a direct session
Inspect delivered caps and runtime status
Repeat with `orbbec/depth/400p_30` and then with `orbbec/preset/480p_30`
Record whether aligned depth-only use works, and whether the grouped preset delivers stable color plus aligned depth

features.description: A standalone Orbbec SDK probe verifies whether aligned `480p` depth can be produced from a depth-only request on the connected device
steps: On probe branch `orbbec-depth-480p-probe`, build the standalone Orbbec probe
Run the probe against the connected Orbbec device
Verify no native `640x480` depth profile is listed
Verify depth-only forced software and hardware D2C both produce `640x480` depth frames with zero delivered color frames
Verify color+depth D2C cases also produce `640x480` depth

features.description: A standalone Orbbec SDK probe verifies whether the connected device exposes aligned `720p` depth or a distinct aligned `800p` depth mode
steps: On probe branch `orbbec-depth-480p-probe`, build the standalone Orbbec probe
Run the extended probe against the connected Orbbec device
Verify the native depth profile list contains `1280x800` but not `1280x720`
Verify `getD2CDepthProfileList` returns no depth profiles for color `1280x720@30` in software and hardware D2C modes
Verify forced software and hardware D2C on depth-only `1280x800@30` still deliver `1280x800` depth frames rather than a distinct aligned output

features.description: An operator can alias a discovered device and then use the aliased name in exact URIs for direct sessions or app routes
steps: Inspect the catalog for a discovered device default name
Call the device alias endpoint
Verify GET /api/devices shows the aliased public name
Verify later session or app-source requests can use the aliased name in the URI

features.description: A user can create a direct logical session through POST /api/sessions without going through the app layer
steps: Call POST /api/sessions with one listed exact URI
Verify the session response is active
Verify the returned payload includes session and exact stream metadata
Verify the session can be inspected through GET /api/sessions/{id}

features.description: A thin opener command can still create a direct session from one exact URI
steps: Run the opener binary with one exact URI
Observe the created session id
Inspect the created session through REST
Verify the same flow is representable through POST /api/sessions

features.description: Two direct sessions can reuse shared capture while exposing separate delivery intents
steps: Create an RTSP-oriented session from a non-local or `rtsp://` source so delivery infers to `rtsp`
Create an IPC-oriented session for the same locally resolved exact source so delivery infers to `ipc`
Inspect runtime status
Verify shared capture reuse is visible while each logical session remains distinct

features.description: A stopped direct session can survive backend exit and be rehydrated explicitly after restart
steps: Create and stop a direct session
Stop the backend
Restart the backend with the same database
Inspect the persisted session record
Call POST /api/sessions/{id}/start
Verify a fresh runtime session is active

features.description: An idle app can connect without initial sources and expose an app id for later route connection
steps: Start an app without any initial source URI
Call GET /api/apps
Find the running app record
Verify the app id can be used for later route and source operations

features.description: A running idle app can declare multiple independent video-purpose routes after startup
steps: Create or locate an idle app
Create route yolov5 with media expectation video
Create route yolov8 with media expectation video
List app routes
Verify both routes are present and distinct

features.description: A running idle app can declare related routes for grouped hardware after startup
steps: Create or locate an idle app
Create route orbbec/color with media expectation video
Create route orbbec/depth with media expectation depth
List app routes
Verify both routes are present and distinct

features.description: A running idle app can receive a later exact video URI connection bound to a declared route
steps: Start an idle app and declare route yolov5
Connect a listed compatible exact video URI with route yolov5
Verify the source record is created
Verify a logical session is linked
Verify the app receives callbacks on the declared route

features.description: A running idle app can receive a later exact depth URI connection bound to a declared route
steps: Start an idle app and declare route orbbec/depth
Connect a listed URI such as insightos://host/desk-rgbd/orbbec/depth/480p_30 with route orbbec/depth
Verify the source record stores the resolved exact stream identity
Verify the app receives callbacks on the declared route

features.description: A running app can bind one declared route to an already-running IPC-capable direct session
steps: Create a direct session from a locally resolved `insightos://` URI so delivery infers to `ipc`
Create or locate an idle app with a compatible route
Create one app-source bind using the existing `session_id` plus `route`
Verify the app receives callbacks on the attached route without recreating the session

features.description: A running app can bind one grouped target to an already-running grouped IPC session
steps: Create a grouped direct session from a locally resolved grouped preset URI so delivery infers to `ipc`
Create or locate an idle app with routes such as orbbec/color and orbbec/depth
Create one app-source bind using the existing `session_id` plus `route_grouped`
Verify the app receives callbacks on both routes without separately managing `/color` and `/depth`

features.description: One app can run a video route plus related grouped-source routes at the same time with independent routing
steps: Create one app
Declare one video route, one orbbec/color route, and one orbbec/depth route
Connect one video URI plus grouped-source URIs for color and depth
Verify all routed sources remain active independently

features.description: One app can declare ordinary color and depth routes separately and activate them together through one grouped preset `route_grouped` bind
steps: Create one app with routes orbbec/color and orbbec/depth
Connect a grouped preset URI such as insightos://host/desk-rgbd/orbbec/preset/480p_30 with route_grouped orbbec
Verify the backend records one grouped source bind
Verify ordinary route callbacks still fire independently on both routes
Verify no extra SDK-only frame-merge helper is required for the grouped preset flow

features.description: The same exact URI can be reused across multiple consumers and should deliver the same frame sequence when shared delivery is reused
steps: Bind the same exact URI into multiple consumers
Inspect runtime status
Verify shared delivery reuse is visible when applicable
Verify the consumers observe the same frame sequence

features.description: The same URI with different delivery intents remains distinct delivery sessions while sharing capture when possible
steps: Create one `ipc` session and one `rtsp` session for the same exact stream
Inspect runtime status
Verify separate delivery sessions exist
Verify shared capture reuse is visible

features.description: A single-route app can launch with one bare exact URI argument and immediately consume from it
steps: Build or run an app binary that declares exactly one route
Pass one exact URI as a CLI argument
Verify the CLI connects that URI to the declared route
Verify the app receives the expected route callbacks

features.description: A single-grouped-target app can launch with one bare grouped preset URI argument and immediately consume from it
steps: Build or run an app binary that declares grouped routes such as orbbec/color and orbbec/depth under one grouped target
Pass one grouped preset URI such as insightos://host/desk-rgbd/orbbec/preset/480p_30 as a CLI argument
Verify the CLI connects that URI to the grouped target without requiring separate `/color` and `/depth` inputs
Verify the app receives the expected grouped route callbacks

features.description: A multi-route app can launch from CLI by connecting exact URIs to route names explicitly
steps: Build or run an app that declares multiple routes
Launch it with explicit route-to-URI CLI connections
Verify each route receives the correct source
Verify no post-launch REST injection is required for that startup path

features.description: App-source responses expose route metadata and resolved exact stream identity clearly enough for the SDK to attach
steps: Create an app and declare a route
Connect a compatible source
Inspect the source response
Verify route, uri, delivery_name, resolved_exact_stream_id, resolved_source_variant_id, resolved_source_group_id when present, and session metadata are present

features.description: Stopping and restarting an app source preserves the durable app-source declaration while recreating runtime state
steps: Create an app, route, and source
Call the app-source stop endpoint
Verify the app-source row remains present and moves to stopped
Call the app-source start endpoint
Verify a fresh runtime session is linked to the same app-source record

features.description: A route binding can be changed at runtime without destroying the durable app record
steps: Create an app, route, and initial source
Call the route rebind endpoint with a replacement URI
Verify the durable route binding points at the replacement
Verify status and callbacks reflect the new source

features.description: The browser UI lets a user move from exact stream catalog inspection to app creation, route declaration, and source connection
steps: Open the browser UI
Inspect the listed exact URIs
Create an app
Create one or more routes
Connect a listed URI to a selected route
Verify the routed source appears in the app detail view

features.description: The browser UI can show persisted app and source declarations after backend restart and let the user restart them explicitly
steps: Create an app, route, and source from the UI
Restart the backend with the same database
Reload the UI
Verify the persisted app and source declarations are still listed
Restart the source from the UI
Verify status updates reflect the fresh runtime session
